nodes.name: zterm_terminal
nodes.responsibility: Terminal emulation model and PTY management for shell session handling

nodes.name: model
nodes.responsibility: Core terminal emulation model with ANSI processing and grid state management

nodes.name: ansi
nodes.responsibility: ANSI escape sequence processing and interpretation

nodes.name: escape_sequences
nodes.responsibility: ANSI/VT100 escape code parsing, CSI handlers, and OSC processing

nodes.name: grid
nodes.responsibility: Terminal grid state: character cells, attributes, scroll regions

nodes.name: BlockId
nodes.responsibility: Unique identifier for terminal output blocks/prompts

nodes.name: BlockIndex
nodes.responsibility: Positional index into block structure for fast lookups

nodes.name: mode
nodes.responsibility: Terminal modes: application mode, insert/replace, mouse tracking, etc.

nodes.name: mouse
nodes.responsibility: Mouse event handling and encoding for xterm protocol

nodes.name: indexing
nodes.responsibility: Efficient indexing and position management in terminal grid

nodes.name: shell
nodes.responsibility: Shell session management and command execution coordination

nodes.name: shared_session
nodes.responsibility: Shared session state across terminal instances

hyperedges.name: ANSI Terminal Emulation
hyperedges.responsibility: Full xterm-compatible terminal emulator with escape sequence interpretation

hyperedges.name: Block/Prompt Management
hyperedges.responsibility: Logically group terminal output into blocks for UI interaction

hyperedges.name: Shell Session Coordination
hyperedges.responsibility: Manage shell lifecycle and state sharing across UI components

design_patterns.rationale: ansi → escape_sequences → grid separates parsing from state management
BlockId + BlockIndex enable logical grouping of terminal output beyond raw grid cells
TermMode + KeyboardModes decouple emulation behavior from core model

architectural_rationale.ansi_focus: ANSI/VT100 escape sequence support enables compatibility with curl, vim, less, etc.
architectural_rationale.grid_state: Cell-based grid model matches terminal semantics and enables efficient updates
architectural_rationale.block_abstraction: Blocks group related output for Zterm's command-driven workflow, not needed in standard terminals
